seoTitle: Port Augusta vs Port Bonython — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Augusta and Port Bonython. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Augusta vs Port Bonython: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Augusta and Port Bonython.
heading: Port Augusta vs Port Bonython — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Augusta, South Australia and Port Bonython, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Bonython has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Port Augusta (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Augusta or Port Bonython safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Augusta has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Port Bonython scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Augusta scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Port Bonython scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Augusta or Port Bonython?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Bonython has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
